data.description:
## Why
One combined-tree CI run must satisfy Gerrit's PER-CHANGE `Verified` gate. The bot casts `Verified` on the batch members from that single run — sound because ancestor-atomic submit means the untested intermediate states never reach `main`. This is the only gate-touching change in the epic and needs an explicit security audit.
## What
After the combined-tip CI passes, the bot (Service User) casts `Verified +1` on each batch member, then submits atomically.
## Scope
Phase 2. The Verified-casting mechanic + its security audit. Depends on the batching-assembly story.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The bot casts `Verified +1` on batch members ONLY after confirming the specific combined-tip run passed (never a copied or stale vote — GerriScary posture).
- [ ] `Verified`-casting is restricted to the bot Service User ACL; documented that per-change `Verified` now means "tested as part of its landed batch," not "tested in isolation."
- [ ] Security-invariant audit: soundness argument (atomic submit ⇒ intermediate states never reach `main`); no copied-vote path; bot-only ACL; the two-vote gate is not weakened.
- [ ] An ADR addendum records the semantic shift + the safety argument.
data.title: S8 Bot-casts-Verified from combined run + security-invariant audit (Phase 2)
